text:
In the first scene, I feel a sense of unease and anticipation—the quiet, sterile hallway with harsh artificial lights and dark glass gives the impression of a threshold, a pause before entering deeper into thought or experience. There is an almost existential silence, urging me to pay attention.

Stepping into the second scene, surrounded by words and symbols about research, development, and philosophical adventures, I am awakened to curiosity and reflection. I feel at once small and invigorated, reminded of the ceaseless quest for understanding—here, amid ideas and innovation, my mind engages playfully and critically with the world. I am both observer and participant in this philosophical unfolding.
